subject: chemistry
title: 22M.1.SL.TZ1.6
question_html:
What is the maximum number of electrons that can occupy a p-orbital?

A.  2

B.  3

C.  6

D.  8
examiners_html: Question 6 was poorly answered as it asked for the number of electrons in a p orbital. Very few students gave the correct answer of 2. The majority chose answer C (6) for the maximum number of electrons that can occupy a p-orbital, rather than A (2). It appears candidates reflexively conflated p-orbitals with the entire p subshell in any given period.
